source_ref: PDF page 1, printed page 137, section 'The Federal Funds Group'
concept_summary: The Federal funds group covers Government financial transactions that are not legally required to be recorded in trust funds and represents the larger share of the budget relative to the trust funds group.
fund_group_implication: A Table 1.4 federal-funds row is a broad accounting group, not the same thing as the general fund alone.
legal_dedication_implication: Federal-funds status alone does not prove whether a specific collection is dedicated or undedicated; the source notes that this group includes both the general fund and certain dedicated special or revolving funds.
appropriation_implication: Unknown at the broad group level; appropriation treatment depends on the account or fund type.
public_caveat: Do not describe every federal-funds dollar as general-fund revenue or as legally unrestricted without narrower account support.
notes: Anchor verified against AP13 extracted text on 2026-06-21.

source_ref: PDF page 1, printed page 137, section 'The Federal Funds Group'
concept_summary: The general fund is used for general Government purposes, receives collections not dedicated to another fund, and includes virtually all income taxes plus many excise taxes.
fund_group_implication: General fund is a subset of the Federal funds group and is the relevant concept for ordinary income-tax receipts when no separate dedication source is cited.
legal_dedication_implication: Supports treating collections not dedicated to another fund as general fund collections; it does not identify statute-specific exceptions.
appropriation_implication: The source describes general-purpose use but does not by itself define a program-level appropriation rule.
public_caveat: Ordinary income-tax receipts can be described as general-fund revenue only with this budget-concept caveat and without claiming a direct program destination.
notes: Anchor verified against AP13 extracted text on 2026-06-21.

source_ref: PDF page 1, printed page 137, section 'The Federal Funds Group'
concept_summary: Special funds are part of the Federal funds group and record collections and disbursements when law dedicates Federal fund collections to a particular program.
fund_group_implication: Special funds should be distinct from both the general fund and broad federal-funds totals when the source supports that level.
legal_dedication_implication: Supports a dedicated label only where the relevant law requires collections to be dedicated to a program; broad category knowledge is not account-level proof.
appropriation_implication: AP13 states that money in special fund receipt accounts must be appropriated before it can be obligated and spent.
public_caveat: A special-fund label means legal dedication needs source support and does not mean a taxpayer payment can be traced directly to spending.
notes: Anchor verified against AP13 extracted text on 2026-06-21.

source_ref: PDF page 1, printed page 137, section 'The Federal Funds Group'
concept_summary: Revolving funds conduct continuing cycles of business-like activity, receive proceeds from sales of products or services, and record those proceeds in expenditure accounts as offsetting collections rather than receipt accounts.
fund_group_implication: Revolving-fund activity should not be treated as ordinary receipt revenue; it can affect gross and net outlay presentation.
legal_dedication_implication: The source places revolving funds among fund types with legally dedicated collections for specific purposes, but specific funds still require their own authority source.
appropriation_implication: AP13 states the proceeds are generally available for obligation and expenditure without further legislative action.
public_caveat: Offsetting collections from revolving funds reduce or finance reported program activity and are not the same as general tax receipts.
notes: Anchor verified against AP13 extracted text on 2026-06-21.

source_ref: PDF page 1, printed page 137, section 'The Trust Funds Group'
concept_summary: The trust funds group consists of funds designated by law as trust funds; like special and revolving funds, they receive collections dedicated by law for specific purposes.
fund_group_implication: Trust-fund rows should remain distinct from general-fund and federal-funds rows and should not be merged into ordinary income-tax receipts.
legal_dedication_implication: Supports dedicated treatment for trust-fund collections at the concept level, subject to account-specific source review.
appropriation_implication: The source says trust-fund income must be used for purposes specified in law, but this record does not define whether any particular expenditure is automatic or annual-appropriation based.
public_caveat: Trust-fund status indicates statutory budget treatment, not private ownership and not direct taxpayer-to-program tracing.
notes: Anchor verified against AP13 extracted text on 2026-06-21.

source_ref: PDF page 2, printed page 138, section 'The Trust Funds Group'
concept_summary: AP13 distinguishes Federal trust funds from common private trust usage and explains that, for most Federal trust funds, the Government owns and manages assets and can change future collections, payments, or purposes by changing law.
fund_group_implication: Trust-fund rows need a public caveat so users do not infer private trust ownership or immutable beneficiary property rights from the fund label.
legal_dedication_implication: The fund label is statutory, but the source cautions that trust designation can be arbitrary relative to similar Federal funds.
appropriation_implication: This caveat does not determine appropriation status; it limits the meaning of the trust-fund label.
public_caveat: Use 'trust fund' as a Federal budget-accounting term unless a source specifically establishes private-trust-style custody.
notes: Anchor verified against AP13 extracted text on 2026-06-21.
